_description: HMECyst cyst-forming -- A07. ~250 cells in shell. lumen_fraction=0.78 creates genuine hollow lumen. Key biology: highly regular spacing (Ripley ~2.7 vs 1.7 control), more neighbours at 50 um (n~63 vs 50), smaller crystal distance (17.3 um), smaller cells (r~12.6 um). Topology is the discriminator vs control.

_calibration.source: Ong et al. 2025, Nature Methods, DOI: 10.1038/s41592-025-02685-4 — spatial topology dataset, sample A07 (cyst-forming)
_calibration.n_cells_measured: ~892 of 1784 total (8 samples split across A01/A07)
_calibration.spatial_character: Crystal-like regular shell monolayer around hollow lumen — cyst architecture
_calibration.model_limitation: lumen_fraction=0.78 creates a genuine hollow exclusion zone in the packing step. No necrotic workaround needed.
_calibration.imaging_microscope: Nikon Ti2-E AX confocal, 25x Plan Apo Lambda S Sil 1.05NA

_ncells_target: ~40-60 cells in shell (lumen takes 78% of radius)
